rxn_class_summary: Photoexcited Gd(III)-catalyzed enantioselective dearomative [2π+2σ] cycloaddition of naphthalenes with bicyclo[1.1.0]butanes to give chiral tricyclic BCHs

key_intermediates.name: Excited S1 singlet Gd-bound naphthalene complex (1*I)
Excited T1 triplet Gd-bound naphthalene complex (3*I)
Triplet 1,4-biradical from radical addition of 3*I to BCB 2a (intermediate II)

electron_flow: Gd(III) chelates the acylpyridine directing group of the naphthalene and red-shifts its absorption; visible-light excitation gives the singlet S1 complex, which undergoes ISC to a triplet that adds across the central C–C σ-bond of the BCB to form a triplet 1,4-biradical, followed by a second ISC and radical recombination to close the cyclobutane and deliver the dearomatized product after ligand exchange with fresh naphthalene.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: 3 equiv suppressed formation of 3a (trace yield), consistent with radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: O2
probe_reagents.observation: 1 atm O2 reduced 3a to trace, supporting triplet/radical involvement

probe_reagents.name: hexa-2,4-diene
probe_reagents.observation: 0.5 equiv → 70% yield; 1 equiv → 52% yield; triplet quencher diminishes yield, supporting triplet naphthalene intermediate

probe_reagents.name: acetyl-naphthalene 10 (R=Me)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without the 2-acylpyridine bidentate DG, confirming necessity of bidentate Gd coordination

probe_reagents.name: benzoyl-naphthalene 11 (R=Ph)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction, confirming bidentate DG required

probe_reagents.name: enantioenriched ligand L1 (variable ee)
probe_reagents.observation: Linear correlation (slope 0.962, R^2=0.9968) between ee of L1 and ee of 3a, indicating a single Gd/L1 species delivers enantioinduction (no nonlinear effect)

notes: Light on/off experiments show reaction halts in the dark, consistent with a photocatalytic (not radical-chain) regime. UV-vis shows that only the Gd(III)+L1+naphthalene+BCB combination absorbs significantly in the visible region, supporting selective excitation of the Gd-bound naphthalene complex rather than free BCB or naphthalene. Linear effect study argues against aggregated Gd species. A singlet-only pathway is disfavored because triplet quenchers (O2, TEMPO, hexa-2,4-diene) strongly suppress the reaction; the authors propose ISC to a triplet biradical followed by a second ISC before ring closure.
